metadata.raw: {"ID":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/id/ssi/2025/406","TITLE":"The Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025","LINK (SELF)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/id/ssi/2025/406","LINK":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/made","LINK (XML)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/made/data.xml","LINK (RDF/XML)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/made/data.rdf","LINK (AKN)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/made/data.akn","LINK (HTML snippet)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/made/data.xht","LINK (Website (XHTML) Default View)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/made/data.htm","LINK (PDF)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/made/data.pdf","LINK (Table of Contents)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/contents/made","AUTHOR":"","UPDATED":"2025-12-18T16:39:05Z","PUBLISHED":"2025-12-18T16:33:56.999695Z","DocumentMainType":"ScottishStatutoryInstrument","YEAR":"2025","NUMBER":"406","ISBN":"'9780111064764'","AlternativeNumber":"","CreationDate":"2025-12-16","SUBJECT":"PUBLIC SERVICE PENSIONS","Supersedes":"","CATEGORY":"PUBLIC SERVICE PENSIONS","SUMMARY":"The Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025 (“the Order”) amends the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Order 2007 (S.S.I. 2007/199) (“the Scheme”). The schedule of the Order makes various amendments to the Scheme relating to retained firefighters. These include amendments to introduce new awards which are payable following the death of a retained firefighter, to extend the deadline for their purchase of service during the extended limited period and to amend the eligibility criteria for some awards and conversions.","":""}

This item of legislation is currently only available in its original format. Scottish Statutory Instruments 2025 No. 406 Public Service Pensions The Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025 Made 16th December 2025 Laid before the Scottish Parliament 18th December 2025 Coming into force 21st February 2026 The Scottish Ministers make the following Order in exercise of the powers conferred by section 34(1), (2) and (4)( 1 ) and section 60(2)(a)( 2 ) of the Fire and Rescue Services Act 2004( 3 ). In accordance with section 34(5) of that Act, the Scottish Ministers have consulted with such persons as they consider appropriate. Citation and commencement 1. This Order may be cited as the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025 and comes into force on 21 February 2026. Amendment of the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Order 2007 2. Schedule 1 of the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Order 2007( 4 ) is amended in accordance with the schedule of this Order. IVAN MCKEE Authorised to sign by the Scottish Ministers St Andrew’s House, Edinburgh 16th December 2025 Article 2 SCHEDULE Amendment of schedule 1 of the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Order 2007 1. In Part 1 (citation and interpretation), in rule 2(1) (interpretation)— (a) in the definition of “extended limited period”( 5 ), in paragraph (a), after “joined” insert “(or, in the case of a retained firefighter opt-out member, rejoined)” , and  (b) after the definition of a “retained firefighter” insert—  “ “ retained firefighter opt-out member ” means a retained firefighter who—  (a) joined this Scheme after 6 April 2006, and  (b) left this Scheme before 31 March 2015, ” .  2. In Part 3 (personal awards), in rule 1A(8) (special member’s ordinary pension)( 6 ), after “Part 11” insert “and rules 17 and 18 of Part 12” . 3. In Part 5 (awards on death)— (a) in rule 1B (death grant for extended limited period)( 7 )—  (i) in paragraphs (2)( 8 ) and (3)( 9 ), for “2025” substitute “2026” , and  (ii) in paragraphs (6)(b), (8) and (9), for “additional”, in each place it occurs, substitute “extended” ,  (b) in rule 1C (additional death grant)( 10 )—  (i) in paragraph (1)(b), after “this Scheme” insert “or was eligible to make an election” , and  (ii) in paragraphs (1)(c)( 11 ), (2)( 12 ) and (3)( 13 ), for “2025” substitute “2026” ,  (c) after rule 2 (post-retirement death grant)( 14 ) insert—  Missed pension lump sum grant 3. — (1) This rule applies where a person—  (a) would have been a special firefighter member if, within the period required by rule 6B(1) ( 15 ) or (12) ( 16 ) of Part 11, they had elected to pay the mandatory special period pension contribution,  (b) would have been eligible to receive special pension payments or additional special pension payments, as applicable, under rule 1A of Part 3, and  (c) died during the period referred to in paragraph (2).  (2) For the purposes of paragraph (1)(c) the period is—  (a) if the person was not employed as a retained firefighter at the time of their death, between 7 April 2000 and 5 April 2006, or  (b) otherwise, between 6 April 2006 and 17 September 2026.  (3) An application for a missed pension lump sum grant may be made by—  (a) the spouse or civil partner of the deceased, or  (b) if the deceased was not married or in a civil partnership at the time of their death, or where their spouse or civil partner has died since the deceased’s death—  (i) a child of the deceased, or  (ii) if the deceased did not have children at the time of their death, or where their children have died since the deceased’s death, the persons who are beneficially entitled to the personal estate of the deceased.  (4) Subject to paragraph (6), the missed pension lump sum grant is a lump sum equal to the value, together with interest, of the pension payments the deceased would have received up to the date of their death if, at the date of their death, they had been a member of this Scheme who had made contributions equivalent to their contributions under rules 6A (Election to purchase service during the limited period or the extended limited period) and 6B (Election to purchase service during the limited period or the extended limited period: supplemental provision) of Part 11.  (5) The interest payable in accordance with paragraph (4) must be calculated in accordance with rule 1A(9) of Part 3 as if references in that rule to paragraph (8) were references to paragraph (4) of this rule.  (6) The amount of the missed pension lump sum grant must be reduced by the value of the deceased’s contributions under rules 6A and 6B of Part 11.  (7) Any application for a grant under this rule must—  (a) be made in writing to the authority who most recently employed the deceased as a firefighter,  (b) be made by the spouse, civil partner, child or personal representative of the deceased, as applicable,  (c) confirm the date on which the deceased took up employment as a retained firefighter,  (d) where the deceased left that employment, confirm the date on which they left,  (e) where the deceased took up employment as a regular firefighter, confirm the date on which they took up that employment,  (f) if the deceased joined this Scheme as a standard member or joined the 1992
